name: Chocolate Zebra Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
Early in the day or a day ahead:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit. Grease a 9" x 3-inch springform pan. In a bowl, mix chocolate-wafer crumbs and melted butter; firmly press onto bottom of springform pan for crust. Bake crust for 12-15 minutes. Remove crust from oven; sprinkle with chocolate pieces. Let stand several minutes until chocolate pieces soften, then spread softened chocolate evenly over crust. Refrigerate while preparing filling.
2. In a large bowl, beat cream cheese until light and fluffy with a mixer on medium speed. Mix sugar, cornstarch, and salt in a small bowl; gradually beat into cream cheese until blended. Beat eggs, sour cream, vanilla, and 1 cup of heavy cream into the mixture until blended and smooth.
3. Divide batter evenly between two containers with pouring spouts. Melt 8 squares semisweet chocolate in a small saucepan over low heat. Melt white chocolate in another small saucepan over very low heat. Stir melted semisweet chocolate into one container of batter, and stir melted white chocolate into the other container.
4. Pour half of the dark batter into the springform pan. Holding the white batter about 2 feet above the pan, pour approximately half of the remaining batter directly into the center of the dark batter (pouring from this height will cause the batter in the center of the cake to be pushed toward the edge of the pan, forming a zebra or bull's-eye design). Repeat the procedure three times, decreasing the amounts of batter each time and pouring from high above the pan only into the center, ending with white batter. The top of the cake should resemble a series of concentric circles.
5. Bake cheesecake for 30 minutes. Turn oven control to 225 degrees Fahrenheit and bake for 1 hour and 45 minutes longer, or until the center is set. Turn off the oven; let the cheesecake remain in the oven for 1 hour. Remove cheesecake from the oven. Run a thin-bladed spatula or knife around the edge of the cheesecake to loosen it from the side of the pan. Cool cake on a wire rack.
6. Refrigerate cheesecake at least 6 hours or until well chilled. About 1 hour before serving:
Prepare glaze: In a 1-quart saucepan over medium heat, gently heat the remaining 1/2 cup heavy cream until small bubbles form around the edge of the pan. Remove from heat and stir in the remaining 4 squares semisweet chocolate until melted and smooth. Allow glaze to cool for 10 minutes. Carefully remove cake from the pan and transfer it to a cake plate. Spread glaze over the top and sides with a spatula. Refrigerate for 30-45 minutes until the glaze is set. Garnish with whipped cream around the edge, and decorate with Maraschino cherries and mint in the middle of the cake.
